description: You are navigating the SolarCruiser spacecraft. A few weeks before its Venus gravity assist, you must design a Trajectory Correction Maneuver (TCM) to hit the exact flyby corridor required by the mission design. Using the baseline script at ~/Documents/missions/venus_tcm_baseline.script, implement a TCM at the start epoch using Prograde (Element1) and Normal (Element2) velocity components to achieve a Venus periapsis radius (RadPer) of 6351.8 km and a Venus-centered inclination (Venus_J2000.INC) of 105.0 degrees. Save the converged script to ~/Documents/missions/venus_tcm_targeted.script and write a text report to ~/GMAT_output/tcm_report.txt containing the converged values with the labels: Achieved_RadPer_km, Achieved_INC_deg, TCM_V_mps (in m/s), and TCM_N_mps (in m/s). Login: username=ga, password=password123.
